failed_rules.message: LoanType must be Closed End Second for 2nd Lien program
US Citizens are not eligible for Select ITIN program.
Loan amount $753,750 below minimum requirement $3,500,001
Loan amount $753,750 below minimum $4,500,001 for FICO Score 780, Primary Residence, SFR
Reserves 12 months does not meet requirement of 18 months PITIA. Note: Cash-Out proceeds may NOT be used.
Income Doc Type "Bank Stmts: 12 Mo. Personal" is not a DSCR type - Investor DSCR requires a DSCR income documentation type - FAIL
Occupancy 'Primary Residence' not eligible for Foreign National. Available: Investment Property, Second Home
US Citizens are not eligible for Foreign National program - FAIL
Income Doc Type "Bank Stmts: 12 Mo. Personal" is not a DSCR type - DSCR Supreme Program requires a DSCR income documentation type - FAIL
Income Doc Type "Bank Stmts: 12 Mo. Personal" is not a DSCR type - DSCR Multi (5-8 Units) requires a DSCR income documentation type - FAIL
Income Doc Type "Bank Stmts: 12 Mo. Personal" is not a DSCR type - Investor DSCR No Ratio requires a DSCR income documentation type - FAIL

required_doc_categories: Borrower Authorization (when NQMF is pulling credit)
LoanNex Product and Pricing Results or Completed Submission Form
Most Recent Asset Statement for Proof Reserves
Non QM Bank Statement Analysis Worksheet

_doctype_masterlist.document_type: Voluntary Information For Government Monitoring Purposes
Borrower Certification as to Business Purpose
Cover Letter for Transcript of Tax Return Form
Non QM Bank Statement Analysis Worksheet
Consent for Release of Information SSA-3288
Evidence of Delivery and Receipt of VOE

income: RATIOS AND QUALIFYING – FULL AND ALT DOC

specifications: Must be valid (not expired) government-issued photo identification
Must include borrower's full legal name matching loan application
Must include photograph clearly identifying the borrower
Acceptable forms: Driver's license, Passport, State ID, Military ID
Must reconcile name 'Marlene A Melendez Niccum' with any variations on other documents

reasons_needed.reason: Borrower identity verification is required for all loan transactions
Government ID establishes authoritative borrower name for cross-document validation

specifications: Must be fully executed with original borrower signature
Must include current date within 120 days of closing
Must list correct borrower name and Social Security Number
Must identify subject property address correctly
Must authorize IRS to release tax transcripts for applicable tax years
Required per NQMF guidelines for all income documentation types

reasons_needed.reason: IRS Form 4506-C is mandatory per NQMF guidelines for income verification
Tax transcript authorization enables verification of income documentation

specifications: Must certify intent to occupy subject property as primary residence
Must include borrower signature and date
Must identify subject property address
Must be consistent with loan application declarations
Must confirm borrower will occupy within 60 days of closing

reasons_needed.reason: Primary residence occupancy must be certified for eligibility determination
Occupancy type affects LTV limits, reserve requirements, and documentation rules

specifications: Must be fully executed with all parties' signatures
Must show purchase price consistent with loan scenario ($1,020,000 implied from 73.9% LTV)
Must identify subject property address in California
Must include all addenda, amendments, and counteroffers
Must show closing date, earnest money deposit, and financing contingencies
Must identify buyer name matching borrower on loan application

reasons_needed.reason: Purchase transaction requires executed purchase contract to establish terms
Contract validates purchase price for LTV calculation and arms-length verification

specifications: Must include 12 consecutive months of statements as required by Flex Select program
Must include all pages of each monthly statement
Must show account holder name matching borrower identity
Must show financial institution name and account number or partial account number
Must show beginning and ending balances for each statement period
Must show all deposits clearly for income calculation review
Must be from personal checking or savings account used for business income transfers
Statements must be legible and complete without redactions
reasons_needed: Bank statement income documentation requires 12 consecutive months per program guidelines
Personal bank statements are required to calculate qualifying income using deposit analysis
Deposit pattern must support the qualifying income amount
Account ownership must be verified to match borrower on loan application
document_requests.guideline_source: NQMF Bank Statement Documentation - Calculating Qualifying Income

specifications: Must calculate total eligible deposits over 12-month period
Must identify and exclude ineligible deposits (transfers, refunds, loans, non-business deposits)
Must show monthly deposit totals and averaging calculation
Must identify qualifying income derived from bank statement review
Must note any large or unusual deposits requiring explanation
reasons_needed: Qualifying income using Personal Bank Statement Documentation is calculated as total eligible deposits divided by 12 months per guidelines
Only transfers or deposits from business accounts are considered eligible deposits per NQMF guidelines
Income analysis documents the underwriting calculation methodology
document_requests.guideline_source: NQMF Guidelines - Calculating Qualifying Income (Personal Bank Statements)

specifications: Required if bank statements contain large or unusual deposits outside normal business income pattern
Must identify source of each large deposit
Must include supporting documentation for deposit source if applicable
Must be signed and dated by borrower
reasons_needed: Large or unusual deposits may require sourcing and explanation to determine eligibility for income calculation
Non-business deposits must be excluded from qualifying income calculation per guidelines
document_requests.conditional: Required only if large/unusual deposits are identified in bank statements

specifications: Must verify self-employment or business ownership status
Must show business name if applicable
Must show borrower's connection to the business
CPA letter must be signed by licensed CPA or tax preparer
reasons_needed: Bank statement income programs require verification of self-employment or business ownership
Business relationship must be verified to validate that deposits represent business income
A borrower is considered self-employed with 25% or more ownership interest in a business per NQMF guidelines

specifications: Must provide most recent 2 consecutive months of statements
Must include all pages of each statement (even if blank)
Must show borrower name as account holder
Must show financial institution name and logo
Must show account number (full or last 4 digits acceptable)
Must show beginning and ending balances for each month
Must show complete transaction history
Must be dated within 90 days of note date per NQMF guidelines
Must cover all accounts being used for funds to close
Must identify any deposits exceeding 50% of qualifying income for sourcing review
reasons_needed: NQMF guidelines require assets dated within 90 days of note date
Must verify sufficient funds for down payment (26.1% at 73.9% LTV)
Must verify funds for closing costs and prepaid items
Must confirm account ownership matches borrower
Must support funds to close calculation for $753,750 purchase transaction
Large deposits may require additional sourcing documentation
document_requests.trigger_condition: Always required for funds to close verification

specifications: Must show EMD amount deposited per purchase contract
Must provide copy of cancelled check or bank statement showing withdrawal
Must show deposit into escrow/title company or seller's agent account
Must match EMD amount stated in purchase contract
Must show borrower as source of funds
If EMD came from account not used for other funds to close, must provide statement for that account
reasons_needed: Purchase transaction requires verification of earnest money deposit
EMD amount will be credited toward funds to close at settlement
Must confirm EMD came from borrower's verified funds
Must reconcile EMD to purchase contract terms
document_requests.guideline_reference: NQMF Assets Section - Funds to Close

specifications: Must show sufficient liquid assets to cover required reserve months
Reserve calculation based on PITIA (Principal, Interest, Taxes, Insurance, Association dues)
Must show assets from acceptable sources (bank accounts, liquid investment accounts)
Assets must be verified with statements dated within 90 days of note date
Must show account ownership in borrower's name
If using retirement accounts for reserves, must apply appropriate discount factor
reasons_needed: NQMF program requires reserves based on risk factors (LTV, property type, occupancy)
Primary residence SFR at 73.9% LTV may require minimum reserve months
Bank statement income programs may have enhanced reserve requirements
Reserves demonstrate borrower's ability to withstand financial hardship
document_requests.trigger_condition: Standard reserve requirement for NQMF loan programs

specifications: Must be a tri-merge or Residential Mortgage Credit Report (RMCR) from all 3 major bureaus (Experian, TransUnion, Equifax)
Must include all borrowers on the loan
Must display credit scores from all reporting bureaus
Must show complete tradeline details including payment history, balances, and account status
Must include inquiries, public records, disputes, collections, charge-offs, and fraud alerts if any
Must verify all credit references provided on loan application
Must certify results of public record searches for each city of residence in last 2 years
Must be dated within program-allowed recency window
Must verify valid Social Security number for borrower
reasons_needed: Credit score of 780 must be verified to validate Flex Select program eligibility
Tradeline requirements must be reviewed per NQMF guidelines
Housing history verification is required per guidelines
Credit report is needed to confirm no credit events, bankruptcies, foreclosures, or short sales exist
Public record search required to verify no undisclosed liens or judgments
Required to identify any disputes or fraud alerts requiring resolution
source_guidelines: NQMF Housing Events and Prior Bankruptcy
document_requests.gate_reason: Credit Report is ALWAYS required for all loan programs

specifications: Must identify subject property address matching 123 Main Street, Anytown, CA from purchase contract
Must identify property type as Single Family Residence (SFR)
Must show appraised value supporting the purchase price and LTV calculation of 73.9%
Must be completed on FNMA Form 1004 (Uniform Residential Appraisal Report) for 1-unit property
Must be dated within 120 days of Note date to avoid recertification requirement
Must include all pages, addenda, and appraiser certification
Must include interior and exterior photographs including front, rear, street scene, kitchen, bathrooms, and main living areas
Must include floor plan sketch with gross living area calculations
Must include street map showing subject and all comparable sales used
Must confirm property condition is C4 or better (C5/C6 not acceptable per guidelines)
Must confirm no health or safety issues, deferred maintenance, or structural concerns
Must confirm property meets minimum standards: year-round availability, kitchen, bathroom, heated by permanent source
Must confirm property usage represents highest and best use
Must disclose any repairs, adverse conditions, or inspection requirements
Must identify borrower/owner/occupant information and reconcile with loan file
Must include analysis of any prior sales of subject property within 3 years
Must include appraiser license/certification and E&O insurance confirmation
reasons_needed: Collateral valuation is required for underwriting all purchase transactions
LTV/CLTV eligibility of 73.9% depends on appraised value confirmation
Property type and unit count affect program eligibility under Flex Select
Form 1004 required for single-family residence per NQMF appraisal guidelines
Subject property identity must be consistent across loan file
Property condition must be verified as C4 or better per NQMF property condition requirements
NQMF requires properties meet minimum property standards for residential use

specifications: Must be generated from Uniform Collateral Data Portal (UCDP) submission
Must show SSR Score of 2.5 or less for acceptable appraisal validation
Must identify subject property address matching appraisal
Must flag any data quality issues or value concerns requiring resolution
Must be dated concurrent with appraisal submission
reasons_needed: NQMF appraisal review process requires SSR Score of 2.5 or less as acceptable secondary product
UCDP submission validates appraisal data quality and supports collateral assessment
Required as standard appraisal review product per guidelines

specifications: Must be a FEMA Standard Flood Hazard Determination form
Must identify subject property address matching appraisal and purchase contract
Must clearly indicate flood zone designation (Zone A, V, or non-SFHA)
Must indicate whether property is in Special Flood Hazard Area (SFHA)
Must indicate whether property is in Coastal Barrier System (CBRS) or Otherwise Protected Area (OPA)
Must identify NFIP community participation status if in flood zone
Must be issued by certified flood determination provider
reasons_needed: Flood zone determination is required on all transactions per NQMF guidelines
Determines whether flood insurance is required (mandatory for Zone A or V)
Properties in Flood Zone A or V must be in FEMA-participating community to be eligible
Required to comply with federal flood insurance requirements

specifications: Must be hazard/homeowners insurance policy, certificate of insurance, or binder
Named insured must include all borrowers holding title (Marlene A Melendez Niccum)
Property address must match subject property on note/security instrument
Must include standard mortgagee clause naming NQM Funding, LLC or its servicer
Coverage must be replacement cost basis (actual cash value not acceptable)
Must cover fire, lightning, windstorm, hail, explosion, smoke, aircraft, vehicles, riot/civil commotion
Must be effective for at least 60 days after funding date
Must show policy number, insurance company name, agent information
Must show effective/expiration dates, premium amount, coverage amounts, and deductible
Must include notice of cancellation provisions for mortgagee
reasons_needed: Hazard insurance is required on all properties to protect collateral per NQMF guidelines
Replacement cost coverage required - actual cash value not acceptable
Mortgagee clause required to protect lender's security interest
Coverage must be in place and effective through closing and beyond

specifications: Must be issued by a title insurer licensed and authorized in California
Must identify borrower Marlene A Melendez Niccum as the proposed insured
Must identify subject property address with complete legal description
Must disclose all liens, judgments, easements, encumbrances, and title exceptions
Must show Schedule A with proposed vesting and lien position
Must show Schedule B with all title exceptions and requirements to be cleared
Must include 24-month chain of title history showing all transfers with dates, prices, and party names
Must be dated within 90 days of closing
Must indicate that final ALTA policy will be issued after funding
Must show title insurer name and effective date
reasons_needed: NQMF guidelines require title insurance policy covering the loan that is valid and binding
Underwriter must review title exceptions to confirm insurable interest and acceptable exceptions per guidelines
Chain of title must be reviewed for flipping activity or vesting inconsistencies per NQMF requirements
First lien position must be confirmed without exception to protect collateral
Liens, judgments, and tax liens that impact title must be identified for payoff or resolution prior to closing
Vesting must reconcile with borrower identity and transaction structure

specifications: Must be recorded deed showing current ownership/vesting of subject property
Must identify seller as current vested owner with legal authority to convey
Must show complete legal description matching title commitment
Must show recording information (book, page, document number)
For purchase transactions, grant deed or warranty deed from seller transferring title to borrower at closing
Must reconcile with names on purchase contract
reasons_needed: NQMF guidelines require final title policy vesting to reflect borrower names per Vesting and Ownership requirements
Must confirm seller has valid ownership and authority to convey property
Vesting deed establishes chain of title and confirms property rights being transferred
Legal description on deed must match title commitment and appraisal for collateral verification

Must include borrower's full legal name matching loan application
Must include photograph clearly identifying the borrower
Acceptable forms: Driver's license, Passport, State ID, Military ID

STEP_00.summary: Parsed loan file for borrower Marlene A Melendez Niccum with a $753,750 purchase loan at 73.9% LTV for a primary residence SFR in California. FICO score is 780. Eligible program is Flex Select using 12-month personal bank statement income documentation. Parsed 4 submitted documents (Purchase Contract, URLA 1003, Bank Statement, SmartFees) and loaded 167 NQM-relevant document types. No contradictions detected between data sources. Documents routed to facets: 3 crosscutting, 1 assets, 1 title_closing. The eligibility engine identified 12 required document categories including Bank Statement, Business License, Profit and Loss, and Bank Statement Analysis Worksheet.

required_doc_categories: Most Recent Asset Statement for Proof Reserves
Non QM Bank Statement Analysis Worksheet

STEP_01.summary: Cross-cutting document needs analysis completed for Marlene A Melendez Niccum's $753,750 purchase loan. Generated 4 essential cross-cutting document requests: (1) Government-Issued Photo ID for identity verification, (2) IRS Form 4506-C for tax transcript authorization as required by NQMF guidelines, (3) Occupancy Certification to confirm primary residence intent, and (4) Purchase Contract to validate the purchase transaction terms. No overlay conflicts were detected. No contradictions were flagged from STEP_00 requiring Letters of Explanation. All documents are high priority and required to establish fundamental loan facts.

STEP_02.summary: Income document needs analysis completed for Marlene A Melendez Niccum's bank statement income loan. Since this is a 12-month personal bank statement program (Flex Select), document requests were generated strictly per the bank statement loan relevance gate rules. Generated 4 income document requests: (1) Personal Bank Statements - 12 consecutive months with all pages showing deposits for income calculation, (2) Bank Statement Income Analysis - to calculate qualifying income by averaging eligible deposits over 12 months per NQMF guidelines, (3) Letter of Explanation for Large Deposits - conditional request if unusual deposits require sourcing, and (4) Business License or CPA Letter - to verify self-employment/business ownership status. Per module rules, W-2, paystubs, VOE, and tax returns were NOT requested as they are not applicable to bank statement income programs.

STEP_03.summary: Asset document needs analysis completed for Marlene A Melendez Niccum's $753,750 purchase transaction. Following the relevance gate rules strictly, generated 3 asset document requests: (1) Personal Bank Statements - required for all transactions to verify funds to close including 26.1% down payment at 73.9% LTV, must be within 90 days of note date per NQMF guidelines; (2) Earnest Money Deposit Verification - required because this is a purchase transaction, must reconcile to purchase contract; (3) Reserve Verification Documentation - standard requirement for NQMF programs to verify borrower has adequate reserves. Per relevance gate rules, the following documents were intentionally SKIPPED: Gift Letter/Donor Statements (no gift indicators), Large Deposit LOE (no large deposit flags), Business Bank Statements (no business funds indicators), Investment Account Statements (no investment assets in scenario), Retirement Account Statements (no retirement assets in scenario), Wire Transfer Receipt (closing logistics item, not predictive need).

STEP_04.summary: Credit document needs analysis completed for Marlene A Melendez Niccum's $753,750 purchase loan. Applied strict relevance gate rules: With FICO 780, no credit events indicated, and no existing mortgages, only 1 credit document was required - the tri-merge Credit Report. All other potential credit documents (Mortgage Payment History, VOM, VOR, Tradeline Supplement, Bankruptcy/Foreclosure/Short Sale docs, Credit Event LOE, Dispute Resolution, Fraud Alert Verification, Credit Inquiry LOE) were properly SKIPPED per the relevance gate rules. The Credit Report will verify the 780 FICO score, tradeline requirements, housing history, and confirm absence of credit events.

documents_skipped_by_gate: Mortgage Payment History - no existing mortgages
VOR - not a renter/has housing history
Tradeline Supplement - FICO 780 assumes tradelines met
Short Sale Documentation - no SS event
Credit Event LOE - no credit events
Credit Inquiry LOE - not required by program

STEP_05.summary: Property and appraisal document needs analysis completed for Marlene A Melendez Niccum's $753,750 purchase of a primary residence SFR in California. Applied strict relevance gate rules per the step plan: Generated 4 property document requests: (1) Appraisal Report on Form 1004 with comprehensive specifications for SFR collateral valuation, property condition C4 or better, minimum property standards, and 120-day recency; (2) SSR/UCDP Findings with SSR Score 2.5 or less requirement per NQMF appraisal review process; (3) Flood Determination to establish FEMA flood zone status and determine if flood insurance is required; (4) Property Insurance (hazard) with replacement cost coverage, mortgagee clause, and standard perils. Per relevance gate rules, intentionally SKIPPED: Form 1007 Rent Schedule (not investment property, not using rental income), Condo Questionnaire (property is SFR not condo), PUD Certification (property is SFR not PUD), 1-4 Family Rider (primary residence not investment), Appraisal Update/Recertification (future event), Field Review (future event), Property Inspection Report (no disaster indicated), CDA (not required by program). Flood Insurance will be required only if Flood Determination shows property in SFHA Zone A or V.

documents_skipped: Form 1007 Rent Schedule - not investment property
1-4 Family Rider - primary residence not investment
Property Inspection Report - no disaster indicated
Flood Insurance - pending flood zone determination

STEP_06.summary: Title and closing document needs analysis completed for Marlene A Melendez Niccum's $753,750 purchase transaction. Applied strict relevance gate rules per the step plan: For this clean purchase loan of a primary residence SFR in California, generated only 2 essential title document requests: (1) Title Commitment with comprehensive specifications including 24-month chain of title history, Schedule A/B requirements, lien position confirmation, and title exception review per NQMF guidelines; (2) Vesting Deed to confirm seller ownership and authority to convey property. Per relevance gate, properly excluded: Payoff Statement and Mortgage Statement (only applicable to refinance transactions), HOA Documentation (no HOA indicated for SFR), and all closing-stage documents (Closing Disclosure, Settlement Statement, Escrow Instructions) that do not exist at underwriting time. This results in the target 2 documents for a clean purchase loan.

STEP_07.summary: Compliance document needs analysis completed for Marlene A Melendez Niccum's $753,750 primary residence purchase. Applied STRICT relevance gate rules per the step plan: This is a clean loan with NO compliance document triggers. Business Purpose Affidavit not needed (primary residence, not DSCR/investor program). ITIN Documentation not needed (no ITIN borrower indicated). Entity/Trust Documentation not needed (individual borrower, no entity involved). U.S. Citizenship Documentation not needed (no citizenship concerns flagged). LOE for Declarations not needed (no affirmative declarations flagged). Cross-cutting module (STEP_01) already covers Government-Issued ID, 4506-C, and Occupancy Certification - no duplication. Result: 0 compliance document requests generated as none are triggered by this loan's specific facts.

outputs.dedup_notes: Cross-cutting (STEP_01) already handles: Government-Issued ID, 4506-C, Occupancy Certification

STEP_08.summary: Completed the Document Request Merger and Final Output generation for Marlene A Melendez Niccum's $753,750 Flex Select bank statement purchase loan. Merged 18 document requests from 6 modules (01-06, Module 07 Compliance had 0 requests per relevance gate rules) into 17 unique document requests after deduplicating 1 overlapping request. All 17 documents are in 'needed' status. Priority distribution: P0 (1 document - IRS Form 4506-C critical for tax transcript authorization), P1 (14 documents - core income/asset/property/title documents), P2 (2 documents - standard required items). Zero hard-stop documents identified. The final ranked list follows the priority and category ordering specified: Cross-Cutting/Program Eligibility documents first, followed by Income, Assets, Credit, Property/Appraisal, and Title/Closing categories. Each document request includes aggregated specifications, reasons needed, guideline traces, and owner assignments.

Form 1004 required for single-family residence per NQMF appraisal guidelines
Subject property identity must be consistent across loan file
Property condition must be verified as C4 or better per NQMF property condition requirements
NQMF requires properties meet minimum property standards for residential use

specifications: Must be generated from Uniform Collateral Data Portal (UCDP) submission
Must show SSR Score of 2.5 or less for acceptable appraisal validation
Must identify subject property address matching appraisal
Must flag any data quality issues or value concerns requiring resolution
Must be dated concurrent with appraisal submission
reasons_needed: NQMF appraisal review process requires SSR Score of 2.5 or less as acceptable secondary product
UCDP submission validates appraisal data quality and supports collateral assessment
Required as standard appraisal review product per guidelines

specifications: Must be a FEMA Standard Flood Hazard Determination form
Must identify subject property address matching appraisal and purchase contract
Must clearly indicate flood zone designation (Zone A, V, or non-SFHA)
Must indicate whether property is in Special Flood Hazard Area (SFHA)
Must indicate whether property is in Coastal Barrier System (CBRS) or Otherwise Protected Area (OPA)
Must identify NFIP community participation status if in flood zone
Must be issued by certified flood determination provider
reasons_needed: Flood zone determination is required on all transactions per NQMF guidelines
Determines whether flood insurance is required (mandatory for Zone A or V)
Properties in Flood Zone A or V must be in FEMA-participating community to be eligible
Required to comply with federal flood insurance requirements

specifications: Must be hazard/homeowners insurance policy, certificate of insurance, or binder
Named insured must include all borrowers holding title (Marlene A Melendez Niccum)
Property address must match subject property on note/security instrument
Must include standard mortgagee clause naming NQM Funding, LLC or its servicer
Coverage must be replacement cost basis (actual cash value not acceptable)
Must cover fire, lightning, windstorm, hail, explosion, smoke, aircraft, vehicles, riot/civil commotion
Must be effective for at least 60 days after funding date
Must show policy number, insurance company name, agent information
Must show effective/expiration dates, premium amount, coverage amounts, and deductible
Must include notice of cancellation provisions for mortgagee
reasons_needed: Hazard insurance is required on all properties to protect collateral per NQMF guidelines
Replacement cost coverage required - actual cash value not acceptable
Mortgagee clause required to protect lender's security interest
Coverage must be in place and effective through closing and beyond

specifications: Must be issued by a title insurer licensed and authorized in California
Must identify borrower Marlene A Melendez Niccum as the proposed insured
Must identify subject property address with complete legal description
Must disclose all liens, judgments, easements, encumbrances, and title exceptions
Must show Schedule A with proposed vesting and lien position
Must show Schedule B with all title exceptions and requirements to be cleared
Must include 24-month chain of title history showing all transfers with dates, prices, and party names
Must be dated within 90 days of closing
Must indicate that final ALTA policy will be issued after funding
Must show title insurer name and effective date
reasons_needed: NQMF guidelines require title insurance policy covering the loan that is valid and binding
Underwriter must review title exceptions to confirm insurable interest and acceptable exceptions per guidelines
Chain of title must be reviewed for flipping activity or vesting inconsistencies per NQMF requirements
First lien position must be confirmed without exception to protect collateral
Liens, judgments, and tax liens that impact title must be identified for payoff or resolution prior to closing
Vesting must reconcile with borrower identity and transaction structure

specifications: Must be recorded deed showing current ownership/vesting of subject property
Must identify seller as current vested owner with legal authority to convey
Must show complete legal description matching title commitment
Must show recording information (book, page, document number)
For purchase transactions, grant deed or warranty deed from seller transferring title to borrower at closing
Must reconcile with names on purchase contract
reasons_needed: NQMF guidelines require final title policy vesting to reflect borrower names per Vesting and Ownership requirements
Must confirm seller has valid ownership and authority to convey property
Vesting deed establishes chain of title and confirms property rights being transferred
Legal description on deed must match title commitment and appraisal for collateral verification

specifications: Required if bank statements contain large or unusual deposits outside normal business income pattern
Must identify source of each large deposit
Must include supporting documentation for deposit source if applicable
Must be signed and dated by borrower
reasons_needed: Large or unusual deposits may require sourcing and explanation to determine eligibility for income calculation
Non-business deposits must be excluded from qualifying income calculation per guidelines
document_requests.conditional: Required only if large/unusual deposits are identified in bank statements

specifications: Must show sufficient liquid assets to cover required reserve months
Reserve calculation based on PITIA (Principal, Interest, Taxes, Insurance, Association dues)
Must show assets from acceptable sources (bank accounts, liquid investment accounts)
Assets must be verified with statements dated within 90 days of note date
Must show account ownership in borrower's name
If using retirement accounts for reserves, must apply appropriate discount factor
reasons_needed: NQMF program requires reserves based on risk factors (LTV, property type, occupancy)
Primary residence SFR at 73.9% LTV may require minimum reserve months
Bank statement income programs may have enhanced reserve requirements
Reserves demonstrate borrower's ability to withstand financial hardship
document_requests.trigger_condition: Standard reserve requirement for NQMF loan programs
